ports.title: Port Usage
ports.empty: No ports in use on this node

danger.denied: Your role is not allowed to perform this action. Contact an administrator.

danger.deleteNodeDesc: The node will be unregistered; its records are kept. Reconnecting requires re-registration.
danger.deleteUserDesc: This action cannot be undone. The user will no longer be able to sign in.
danger.batchKillDesc: Selected instance processes will be killed; unsaved data may be lost.

serverState.notConnectedHint: Make sure the ServerProbe is deployed and connected for this instance (push the embedded probe from the Metrics tab).
serverState.unavailable: State collection did not succeed this time

nodes.name: Name
nodes.maintenanceEnabled: Maintenance mode on; new instances will not be scheduled here
nodes.drainConfirmDesc: This will stop all running instances on node "{{name}}". Continue?
nodes.deleteConfirmDesc: This deregisters node "{{name}}" but keeps its records; reconnecting requires re-registration. Continue?
nodes.deleteOnlineHint: Online nodes cannot be decommissioned; drain and disconnect the Worker first

enroll.addDesc: Issue a one-time install credential; paste the one-line command on the target machine to auto-register and come online.
enroll.nodeNamePlaceholder: Leave blank to auto-report from Worker
enroll.resultDesc: Copy and save it now. The credential is one-time and time-limited; the full command cannot be shown again after closing.
enroll.hint: If no public download source is configured, copy the Worker binary to the target machine first and append --binary <path> (use -Binary <path> on Windows).
enroll.copyFailed: Copy failed, please select and copy manually

instances.name: Name
instances.workDirSystemAssigned: Auto-assigned by the system (read-only after creation)
instances.workDirHint: Working directory; the file manager uses it as the root
instances.dockerImageHint: Container image reference; defaults to Docker Hub. Pulled automatically before start if missing locally
instances.resourceLimitHint: Leave empty for unlimited; injected into the container cgroup on next start
instances.diskLimitHint: Leave empty for unlimited; recorded for display only in this version
instances.resourceLimitDockerOnly: Resource limits require Docker mode (direct/daemon do not support cgroup limits)
instances.resourceLimitSaved: Resource limits saved; effective on next start

instanceBatch.confirmKillDesc: Force kill terminates the process immediately and may lose unsaved data. It will apply to the {{count}} selected instance(s). This action cannot be undone.

schedules.name: Name

backups.restoreConfirm: Restore to this backup? Current data will be overwritten

backups.name: Name
backups.noBaseForIncremental: No full backup to base on yet. Create a full backup first.
backups.deleteHasChildren: This backup is depended on by incremental backups; delete its children first

backupStorages.title: Backup Storage Backends
backupStorages.subtitle: Configure remote storage for backups (S3 / SFTP / WebDAV). Credentials must reference environment variables as ${ENV_VAR}; plaintext is rejected.
backupStorages.name: Name
backupStorages.empty: No backends. Backups are stored on the node locally by default.
backupStorages.endpointHintS3: S3 endpoint, e.g. s3.amazonaws.com or minio.local:9000
backupStorages.inUse: This backend is referenced by backups and cannot be deleted

clientChannels.title: Client Distribution
clientChannels.subtitle: Manage client distribution channels and pull keys. One channel per server; keys let the player-side updater fetch the manifest and artifacts.
clientChannels.channelIdHint: Lowercase letters/digits/hyphens, 2-64 chars, immutable after creation
clientChannels.deleteChannelDesc: This deletes the channel and all its pull keys. This cannot be undone.
clientChannels.rotateConfirmDesc: After rotation the old key is invalidated immediately; already-distributed clients must update to the new key.
clientChannels.revokeConfirmDesc: Clients using this key will no longer be able to fetch updates.
clientChannels.secretDesc: Copy and store it now. You will not be able to view the full key again after closing.
clientChannels.copyFailed: Copy failed, please select and copy manually

clientVersions.subtitle: Version history is admin-only; players only fetch the latest. Rollback republishes old content under a higher version number and never triggers client downgrade protection.
clientVersions.empty: No versions yet. Click "Publish Version" to start.
clientVersions.rollbackConfirmDesc: This republishes that version's content as the latest under a higher version number (version stays monotonic; clients advance normally and are not rejected by downgrade protection).
clientVersions.wizardDesc: Upload client files (checksums computed automatically), set each file's path and sync policy, then publish. This release publishes uncompressed (codec=none).
clientVersions.managedDirsHint: Comma/newline separated. Only within these dirs are local files not listed in the manifest removed (pruning).

clientStats.subtitle: Distribution stats (aggregated from pull tracking and telemetry). Machine codes are untrusted, used only as a stat dimension.

clientGuide.intro: The wedge self-locates before the game starts, loads updater-core, fetches the signed manifest, incrementally updates client resources, then launches the game; on network failure or config error it always launches anyway (fail-static / fail-open) and never blocks startup. Follow the steps below to integrate the updater into your modpack and ship it to players.
clientGuide.step1Title: ① Download the two updater jars
clientGuide.step1Desc: Download wedge.jar (the wedge) and updater-core.jar (the update core).
clientGuide.notEmbedded: Some jars are not embedded (build needs `make embed-client-updater`)
clientGuide.step2Desc: Put both jars and jm-updater.json into the jm-updater subfolder of the game directory:
clientGuide.step3Desc: Below is this channel's config. Replace key with a pull key created in the Pull Keys tab (the plaintext is shown only once at creation); change endpoint to a publicly reachable distribution address.
clientGuide.keyPlaceholder: Create a key in the Pull Keys tab, then paste it here
clientGuide.step4Title: ④ Add the JVM argument in the launcher
clientGuide.step4Desc: Add the line below to the custom JVM arguments in HMCL / PCL2 (relative path, recommended). Launchers cd into the .minecraft directory before starting the game, so the relative path resolves against the game directory; omitting =gameDir lets the wedge read the absolute gameDir from the MC command line's --gameDir.
clientGuide.step4Abs: If your launcher does not cd into .minecraft, use an absolute path:
clientGuide.behaviorFailStatic: Offline / endpoint unreachable → launches with the existing local version (fail-static).
clientGuide.behaviorFailOpen: Missing / invalid config → skips the update and launches directly (fail-open). Never blocks startup.
clientGuide.behaviorProgress: A standalone progress window (bar + speed + ETA) appears during updates; degrades to text in headless environments.
clientGuide.behaviorMultiAgent: Coexists with other -javaagent such as authlib-injector (third-party login), order-independent.
clientGuide.downloadFailed: Download failed (jar may not be embedded)

templates.title: Server Templates
templates.subtitle: Reusable templates for creating instances; deleting a template does not affect instances already created from it
templates.name: Name
templates.deleteDescription: This only removes the template. Instances already created from it are not affected.

runtimeAssets.title: Runtime & Assets
runtimeAssets.subtitle: Global view of JDK runtimes and the artifact library — references broken down by node/instance, with usage and hot/cold visualized
runtimeAssets.jdkMatrixTitle: Node × Version reference matrix (cell = referencing instances)
runtimeAssets.bindMajor: Resolved to this JDK by Java major version
runtimeAssets.jdkInUse: JDK is in use by instances: {{names}}
runtimeAssets.jdkDeleteDescription: Deletable only when no instance references it; in-use deletion is rejected and shows the occupants.
runtimeAssets.name: Name
runtimeAssets.assetInUse: Asset is referenced by {{count}} place(s) and cannot be deleted
runtimeAssets.assetDeleteDescription: Deletable only when reference count is 0; deletion also removes the physical file.
runtimeAssets.assetDeleteReferenced: This asset is referenced by {{count}} place(s); deletion will be rejected. Release the references first.

storage.title: Platform Storage
storage.subtitle: Read-only browsing, usage stats, archive hot/cold and cache cleanup of the Control Plane data root (FHS layout)
storage.adminOnly: Platform storage is accessible to platform administrators only
storage.loadFailed: Failed to load platform storage overview
storage.clearCacheDescription: Removes all temp files under cache/ ({{count}} item(s) · {{size}}). Only the cache is cleared; other directories are untouched. This action cannot be undone.
storage.name: Name

database.title: Database
database.subtitle: Read-only browse of platform database tables and rows; sensitive columns are masked
database.selectTable: Select a table on the left

systemUpdate.title: System Update
systemUpdate.subtitle: Check and upgrade the Control Plane and node Worker binaries. Upgrades are sha256-verified, hot-swapped, and gracefully restarted; running game servers are unaffected under daemon mode.
systemUpdate.notCheckedYet: Click "Check for updates" to fetch the update source and compare component versions.
systemUpdate.notConfigured: Update source (feed_url) is not configured. Set it under the update section of control-plane.yaml, then check again.
systemUpdate.cpUpgradeConfirmDesc: Downloads the new binary, verifies sha256, replaces it, and gracefully restarts the console. The web UI is briefly unavailable during restart and returns on the new version.
systemUpdate.cpUpgradeStarted: Control Plane upgrade started ({{from}} → {{to}}); restarting shortly
systemUpdate.upgradeAllConfirmDesc: Upgrades every online node one by one (serial). Each node downloads, verifies, hot-swaps, and restarts its Worker; game servers stay up under daemon mode.
systemUpdate.nodeUpgradeConfirmDesc: Tells the node to download the new Worker, verify sha256, replace it, and restart. Running game servers stay up under daemon mode.

alerts.title: Alert Management
alerts.name: Name
alerts.noChannelsHint: No channels yet; create one in the Channels tab first
alerts.inappHint: In-app notifications need no external config; alerts appear in the events list.
alerts.envRefRequired: Credential must reference an env var as ${ENV_VAR}
alerts.envRefHint: Sensitive credentials must reference env vars via ${ENV_VAR}; no plaintext stored.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Manage appearance and platform preferences
settings.appearanceDesc: Customize the interface theme and language
settings.moreDesc: More platform options will be available later
settings.platformDesc: Server-side platform config; editable items take effect on save (overriding env/YAML defaults), read-only items show the current effective value
settings.editableHint: Overrides the default immediately after saving.
settings.readOnlyHint: To change these, edit the config file / environment variables and restart.

catDesc.logging: Log level and other runtime logging settings
catDesc.runtime: JDK download mirrors and instance runtime behavior

validation.absPath: Must be an absolute path (e.g. /servers/x or C:/x)
validation.envRef: Must be an environment variable reference ${VAR_NAME}
validation.host: Must be a valid hostname or IP
validation.nonNegativeNumber: Must be a non-negative number (0 means unlimited)
